# Create the iteration organization {#sec-create-orgs}

This chapter is the checklist for creating your iteration organization and populating it from the templates in `gitforsci-dev`. The central `gitforsci` org and the development `gitforsci-dev` org already exist (@sec-three-orgs); you do not create those. The only organization you create here is the one for this cohort.

Throughout, replace the iteration tag `00x` with your own, for example `cis` or `001`.

## GitHub organizations are created in the browser

One manual fact to know up front: GitHub organizations are created through the website, under your account settings, not from the command line. The step below that says "create an organization" means doing it in the browser. Everything after that, once the org exists, can be scripted.

## Step 1: Create the iteration organization

- [ ] Sign in to GitHub as the instructor account.
- [ ] Go to <https://github.com/account/organizations/new> and create a free organization named `gitforsci-00x` (substitute your tag).
- [ ] Confirm you are listed as an owner.
- [ ] Invite your **test-learner account** (@sec-teacher-setup) as a plain member, so you can later verify the member view.

## Step 2: Generate the cohort website

The cohort website is generated from the `gitforsci-dev/website` template into your new org. The full walk-through, including filling in the iteration variables, is in @sec-website. In short:

- [ ] On `gitforsci-dev/website`, use **Use this template** to create a new repository named `website` inside `gitforsci-00x`.
- [ ] Follow @sec-website to fill in the cohort variables, render, and publish.

## Step 3: Create the per-cohort material repositories

Learners need their own copies of the material to work in. These are generated from the exercise and assignment templates in `gitforsci-dev`:

- [ ] For each exercise and assignment template in `gitforsci-dev`, use **Use this template** to create a copy in `gitforsci-00x`.
- [ ] Confirm each copied repository is visible to org members.
- [ ] If learners each need their own copy of a repository, the `ghclass` scripts in step 4 create them; you do not do this by hand.

## Step 4: Invite learners and create their repositories with `ghclass`

Inviting learners and creating per-learner repositories one by one in the web interface does not scale and is error-prone. This work is scripted with the `ghclass` R package (<https://rundel.github.io/ghclass/>), and the scripts live in `gitforsci-dev/admin`.

- [ ] Collect learner GitHub usernames from the pre-course survey (@sec-before).
- [ ] Open the admin project (`gitforsci-dev/admin`) in RStudio and confirm `ghclass` is installed (@sec-teacher-setup).
- [ ] Run the invitation script to invite every learner to `gitforsci-00x` from the username list.
- [ ] If the cohort uses per-learner repositories, run the script that creates them from the templates.
- [ ] Ask learners to accept the email invitation as part of their pre-work (@sec-learner-setup).

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**New educator** — the `ghclass` scripts are the reason the `admin` repository exists in `gitforsci-dev`. You do not write them from scratch; you run the existing scripts against this cohort's username list.
:::

## Step 5: Verify with the test-learner account

Before you consider the setup done, switch to your test-learner browser and confirm, as an ordinary member:

- [ ] The iteration org page is reachable and shows the expected repositories.
- [ ] A material repository can be cloned in RStudio.
- [ ] A commit pushed from the test-learner account appears on GitHub.

If all three pass from the learner's point of view, your organization is ready.

## When to do all this

The setup in this chapter has to land in a sequence that gives learners time to finish the pre-work and gives you time to follow up with anyone who has not. That count-back timeline, with its buffer windows, lives in the pre-flight chapter (@sec-before), since it ties the org and website setup together with the survey, the follow-up windows, and the dry-run into one schedule you work to.

Next, build the website (@sec-website).
